laws.title: Public Law 16-112 | The Contempt of Congress Act
laws.status_basis: Chronology resolves this law as superseded: Pub. L. 31-228 later repealed Public Law 16-112 and reinstated 2 U.S.C. 192 in full. The recovered source bundle is an access-denied page, so no operative text can be reconstructed.

provisions.text_summary: Historical note only; later law repealed the enactment and restored 2 U.S.C. 192.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only a supersession history note; do not create operative Code text.
provisions.evidence: Pub. L. 31-228 repeals Public Law 16-112 and reinstates 2 U.S.C. 192; the recovered file is an access-denied page.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note and migration report treat the enactment as superseded, with the later contempt rule occupying the operative history.

recommended_actions: Retain the supersession history and the restored 2 U.S.C. 192 reference.
Do not infer any operative text from the unreadable source.
Remove boilerplate if present.

laws.title: Public Law 16-113 | The Bing Bong Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It repeals Public Law 15-96 in full and has no self-repeal or sunset, so the repealing statute itself remains current.

provisions.evidence: The act may be cited as the Bing Bong Act of 2023.

provisions.exact_change: Mark Public Law 15-96 repealed in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 15-96 shall be repealed in its entirety.
This resolution takes effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The live note sits in title 50 with boilerplate repeal/supersession prose and leaves PL 15-96 unrepealed.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to PL 15-96 wherever it is codified.
Replace the boilerplate note with a concise note describing PL 16-113 as a current repealing statute.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-114 | Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. Its only substantive effect is an express amendment to PL 15-102 SEC III(e) with determinable strike-and-insert text, and nothing repeals the act itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Title, severability, and effective-date note only.
provisions.evidence: The act may be cited as the Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act.

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 15-102 SEC III(e) with new presidential authority and Senate-notification text.
provisions.exact_change: Strike the old prohibition and insert the new presidential authority, notice, and Senate-consent language.
provisions.evidence: Section III, Subsection e shall now read the following.

current_implementation.detail: The note is in the right subject area, but it only records the act as a statutory note and does not implement the textual amendment to PL 15-102 SEC III(e).

recommended_actions: Apply the SEC III(e) substitution to PL 15-102.
Replace the boilerplate note with an honest amendment note.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-115 | National Security Threat Mandatory Notification to Congressional Leadership Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and fully operative. It creates a notification duty for foreign threats, defines terms, and ties noncompliance to 18 U.S.C. 1512. Nothing repeals it.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Title, severability, and effective-date note only.
provisions.evidence: The act may be cited as the National Security Threat Mandatory Notification to Congressional Leadership Act.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of Congressional Leadership and National Security Threat.
provisions.evidence: Congressional Leadership shall be defined as the Speaker of the House and related leaders.

provisions.text_summary: Mandatory notification duty and onward-sharing rules.
provisions.evidence: A designated individual from the Executive Branch shall notify the Congressional Leadership of foreign threats.

provisions.exact_change: Cross-reference to 18 U.S.C. 1512 only; no textual amendment supplied.
provisions.evidence: Any failure to provide the information is classified as a crime under U.S. Code 1512.

current_implementation.detail: Title 50 is a plausible anchor, but the XML is still boilerplate and does not distinguish the 18 U.S.C. 1512 cross-reference as reference-only.

recommended_actions: Replace the boilerplate note with an honest statutory note under title 50.
Record the 18 U.S.C. 1512 reference as cross-reference only.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-116 | Combatant Command Re-establishment Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It repeals the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48 and re-establishes six Unified Combatant Commands with command-and-control rules. Nothing repeals PL 16-116.

provisions.evidence: This act may also be referred to as the Combatant Command Re-establishment Act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 in full.
provisions.evidence: The OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 shall be repealed in its entirety.
provisions.notes: Caption also references repeal of Public Law 7-48.

provisions.evidence: This Act shall be severable.

provisions.text_summary: Re-establishes six Unified Combatant Commands and makes them the sole commands.
provisions.exact_change: Establish Cyber, SOCOM, STRATCOM, TRANSCOM, EUCOM, and SPACECOM as the sole combatant commands.
provisions.evidence: The following Unified Combatant Commands shall be established in the United States Military.

provisions.text_summary: Reporting chain and operational authority rules.
provisions.exact_change: Reporting chain and operational authority rules.
provisions.evidence: All Combatant Commanders will report directly to the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.
This bill shall supersede and replace any previous laws.
This bill will take effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The note uses title 50, which is a weak anchor for combatant-command law, and it does not apply the repeal-marking for PL 7-48.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48.
Keep the note at the title 2 statutory-note anchor.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-117 | The Clean Up Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It repeals Senate Joint Resolution 2 and 3; the repealing resolution itself remains current.

provisions.evidence: This Joint Resolution may be cited as the Clean Up Act of 2023.

provisions.text_summary: General rationale for repealing Senate Joint Resolutions 2 and 3.
provisions.exact_change: Historical note for the reasoning clause.
provisions.evidence: Senate Joint Resolution 2 and 3 will never be used for their intended purposes.
Senate Joint Resolution 2 ... shall be hereby repealed.
Senate Joint Resolution 3 ... shall be hereby repealed.
This Joint Resolution will take effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The XML note mislabels a current repealing resolution as repealed or superseded and uses a generic title 50 anchor, but the actual target is uncodified joint resolutions.

recommended_actions: Replace the boilerplate note with a concise historical note.
Correct the status to current.
Remove the Trello link.
Exclude PL 16-117 from permanent Code.

laws.title: Public Law 16-118 | The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The authoritative source text is readable and operative. It repeals H.R. 8013 Section III and replaces that section with a new Capitol Police Board structure and powers. Nothing repeals PL 16-118.

provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023.
The following section of H.R. 8013 shall be repealed in its entirety; Section III.

provisions.text_summary: Substitutes a new Capitol Police Board section with membership and powers.
provisions.exact_change: Replace Section III with the new Capitol Police Board membership and powers.
provisions.evidence: Section III shall now state the following.
This Resolution will take effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: The live note is under title 18, but the source is the Capitol Police Board reorganization text and the operative target belongs under title 2.

recommended_actions: Apply the repeal/substitution to the Capitol Police Board text at the title 2 anchor.
Re-anchor from title 18 to title 2.
Remove the Trello link.
